title: DSDS Pattern Definitions
description: Defines documents for interaction patterns. A pattern is a repeated answer to a common UX problem — navigation, error messaging, form validation, empty states, loading, and onboarding. A pattern document holds the identity of a multi-component solution. The `metadata` object holds optional fields like status, tags, and links. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries.

pattern.description: A repeated, multi-component solution to a common UX problem. Patterns sit above individual components. They show how those components combine to meet a user need. The top level holds its identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlock` array holds structured docs as typed entries: `interactions` for flow steps, `guidelines` for rules, `accessibility` for a11y specs, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a pattern.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the pattern (e.g., 'error-messaging', 'navigation', 'empty-state', 'form-validation'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Error Messaging', 'Navigation', 'Empty State').

description.description: What this pattern is, the UX problem it solves, and how its components compose to meet a user need. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this pattern, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Patterns accept the pattern-specific kinds (interactions) plus the general kinds (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display. Interaction entries rely on order to show the flow over time. List the components a pattern uses in the `links` metadata field (kind 'component', name, and role), not in a dedicated block kind.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only. This is a separate space that accepts the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation limits, ways to tell confusable entities apart, and machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
